name: PAN Card (Permanent Account Number)
description: A 10-character alphanumeric tax identity number issued by the Income Tax Department. Mandatory for filing income tax returns, opening a bank/demat account, and for most financial transactions above prescribed limits. PAN 2.0 (unified ITD portal with dynamic QR) was announced in Nov 2024 but the unified portal is not fully live yet; Protean (formerly NSDL) and UTIITSL remain the issuing portals.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen, NRI, HUF, firm or company. No age limit; minors apply through a representative assessee.
documents: Aadhaar card (mandatory for new individual PAN, must be linked to an active mobile number)
Proof of identity (Aadhaar / Voter ID / Passport / Driving Licence)
Proof of address (Aadhaar / utility bill not older than 3 months / bank statement)
Proof of date of birth (Aadhaar / Birth certificate / SSC memo)
Recent passport-size photograph (for physical mode)
Signature on white paper (for physical/scanned mode)
online_steps: For a free Instant e-PAN: go to the Income Tax e-Filing portal and select 'Instant e-PAN' under Quick Links (only for adults with a valid Aadhaar, active linked mobile, and no PAN allotted earlier).
Otherwise open the Protean TIN portal and choose Form 49A (Indian citizen) under 'New PAN'.
Fill personal details, AO code (auto-suggested by pin code) and select paperless (Aadhaar e-KYC / e-Sign) or physical document submission.
Upload photo, signature and proof documents if using scanned mode.
Pay the fee online (net banking / card / UPI) and note the 15-digit acknowledgement number.
Complete Aadhaar OTP authentication or e-Sign to finish paperless submission.
Track using the acknowledgement number on the Protean status page; e-PAN is emailed and the physical card is dispatched to the registered address.
offline_steps: Collect Form 49A from any PAN facilitation centre, MeeSeva centre or download it from the Protean/UTIITSL site.
Fill the form in block letters, affix two passport photos and sign across the photo.
Attach self-attested copies of identity, address and DOB proof.
Submit at a nearby MeeSeva / TIN-FC / UTIITSL PAN centre and pay the fee in cash.
Collect the acknowledgement slip and track the status with the acknowledgement number.

breakdown: Physical document submission (Indian address): ₹107 incl. GST
Paperless / e-KYC mode (Indian address): ₹101 incl. GST
e-PAN only, physical submission: ₹72
e-PAN only, paperless: ₹66
Foreign communication address: ₹1,017 (physical) / ₹1,011 (paperless)
Reprint without changes: ₹50
Instant e-PAN via Income Tax e-Filing portal: free
fees.notes: MeeSeva / private centres levy an extra service charge of roughly ₹20–₹100.

processing_time: Instant e-PAN in about 10 minutes; regular e-PAN in 24–48 hours; physical card in 10–15 working days
common_issues: Name mismatch between PAN and Aadhaar causes rejection — the name must match Aadhaar exactly.
Mobile number not linked to Aadhaar blocks the e-KYC OTP step.
PAN becomes inoperative if not linked to Aadhaar.

name: Aadhaar Enrolment (New)
description: A 12-digit unique identity number issued by UIDAI based on biometric and demographic data. Required for almost every welfare scheme, subsidy, ration card and bank KYC in Andhra Pradesh.
eligibility: Any resident of India, including infants (children under 5 get a Baal Aadhaar with no biometrics; mandatory biometric update at ages 5 and 15).
documents: Proof of identity (Passport / PAN / Voter ID / school ID)
Proof of address (ration card / electricity bill / rental agreement / bank passbook)
Proof of date of birth (birth certificate / SSC memo / passport)
For a child: parent's Aadhaar plus the child's birth certificate
Head-of-family Aadhaar if enrolling under HOF-based relationship proof
online_steps: New Aadhaar enrolment cannot be completed fully online — biometrics must be captured in person.
Use the 'Book an Appointment' service on the UIDAI portal or the mAadhaar app to reserve a slot at an Aadhaar Seva Kendra.
Fill the pre-enrolment form online and note the appointment ID.
Visit the centre with originals, complete photo, iris and fingerprint capture.
Collect the acknowledgement slip with the 14-digit Enrolment ID (EID) and track status on the UIDAI 'Check Aadhaar Status' page.
Download the e-Aadhaar PDF once generated (password is the first 4 letters of your name in capitals plus year of birth).
offline_steps: Walk in to the nearest Aadhaar Seva Kendra, MeeSeva centre, Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or designated post office / bank branch.
Fill the enrolment form and hand over the supporting documents.
Complete biometric capture (10 fingerprints, both irises, photograph).
Verify the details on the printed acknowledgement before leaving — corrections later cost money.

breakdown: First-time enrolment: free
Mandatory biometric update at age 5 and 15: free
Demographic update (name/address/DOB/gender): approx. ₹50
Biometric update (other than mandatory): approx. ₹100
Colour A4 printout / reprint at a centre: approx. ₹30
fees.notes: UIDAI revises these charges periodically and some online document-update windows are made free for limited periods — confirm the current circular on uidai.gov.in before quoting a price to a citizen.

processing_time: Aadhaar generated in 7–30 days from enrolment; PVC card delivered in about 15 days
common_issues: Enrolment rejected when the address proof is not in the applicant's own name and no HOF linkage is given.
Children who miss the mandatory biometric update at 5 or 15 may find the Aadhaar deactivated.

name: Aadhaar Update / Correction
description: Update name, date of birth, gender, address, mobile number, email or biometrics on an existing Aadhaar. Address and document updates can be done online; mobile number and biometric changes require a visit.
eligibility: Any Aadhaar holder. Name can be changed twice in a lifetime, gender once, and DOB once.
documents: Original Aadhaar number
Valid document from the UIDAI accepted-documents list matching the field being changed
For online updates the mobile number must already be registered with Aadhaar
online_steps: Log in to myAadhaar on the UIDAI portal using your Aadhaar number and OTP.
Choose 'Update Aadhaar Online' and select the field (address / document update).
Enter the new value in English and Telugu and upload the supporting document as a colour scan.
Pay the update fee online where applicable and note the 14-digit Update Request Number (URN).
Track the URN under 'Check Request Status'; download the revised e-Aadhaar once approved.
offline_steps: Visit an Aadhaar Seva Kendra, MeeSeva centre or Sachivalayam with the original document.
Fill the Aadhaar Update/Correction form and submit for operator verification.
Provide biometric authentication; mobile number and biometric updates are only possible here.
Pay the fee and keep the acknowledgement slip with the URN.

breakdown: Demographic update: approx. ₹50
Biometric update: approx. ₹100
Online document update: often free during UIDAI's periodic free windows
fees.notes: Verify current UIDAI charges — they were revised recently and centres frequently overcharge.

processing_time: Usually 7–15 days; up to 90 days for contested name or DOB changes
common_issues: Operators overcharge for mobile-number linking — the published UIDAI rate is far lower than what many centres demand.
Update rejected if the uploaded document does not exactly match the requested new value.

name: Voter ID Card (EPIC) — New Registration
description: Enrolment in the electoral roll and issue of an Elector's Photo Identity Card by the Election Commission of India. Applications use Form 6; corrections use Form 8. Handled through the Voter Services Portal, which replaced the old NVSP flow.
eligibility: Indian citizen, aged 18 or above on the qualifying date, ordinarily resident in the constituency.
documents: Aadhaar card
Proof of age (birth certificate / SSC memo / passport / PAN)
Proof of ordinary residence (electricity or water bill / ration card / rental agreement / bank passbook)
Recent passport-size colour photograph
Existing EPIC number if shifting from another constituency
online_steps: Register on the Voter Services Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or the Voter Helpline app with your mobile number.
Select 'Fill Form 6' for a new voter, or Form 8 for correction/shifting.
Enter personal details, choose your Andhra Pradesh district, assembly constituency and polling area.
Upload the photograph, age proof and address proof.
Submit and note the reference ID.
A Booth Level Officer (BLO) visits for field verification; track the reference ID on the same portal and download the e-EPIC once approved.
offline_steps: Collect Form 6 from the BLO, the Electoral Registration Officer (Tahsildar) office or a MeeSeva centre.
Attach self-attested copies of the age and address proof plus a photograph.
Submit to the BLO of your polling booth or at the ERO office in the Mandal/Tahsildar office.
Field verification is done by the BLO; the printed EPIC is delivered by post or collected from the BLO.

breakdown: Registration and EPIC: free
MeeSeva service charge for form submission: approx. ₹35
fees.notes: There is no government fee for voter registration.

processing_time: Typically 15–30 days after BLO verification; e-EPIC downloadable immediately after approval
common_issues: Duplicate entries when a voter registers in a new district without filing Form 7 to delete the old entry.
Application lapses if the BLO cannot find the applicant at the stated address during verification.

name: Indian Passport (Fresh / Re-issue)
description: Travel document issued by the Ministry of External Affairs through Passport Seva Kendras and Post Office Passport Seva Kendras. Andhra Pradesh applications are processed under the Regional Passport Office, Visakhapatnam or Vijayawada depending on district.
eligibility: Indian citizens of any age. Minors under 18 get a 5-year or till-18 validity passport.
documents: Aadhaar card (accepted as combined proof of identity, address and DOB)
Proof of present address (Aadhaar / electricity bill / bank statement / rental agreement)
Proof of date of birth (birth certificate / SSC memo / PAN / Aadhaar)
Annexure as applicable (Annexure E self-declaration for most adults; Annexure D for minors)
Old passport with self-attested first two and last two pages, for re-issue
Non-ECR proof such as class 10 certificate if claiming ECNR
online_steps: Register on the Passport Seva portal (passportindia.gov.in) and log in.
Select 'Apply for Fresh Passport / Re-issue of Passport' and fill the form online or upload the downloaded e-form XML.
Choose Normal or Tatkaal scheme and the number of pages (36 or 60).
Click 'Pay and Schedule Appointment' — payment is mandatory before booking a slot at your PSK or POPSK.
Print the Application Receipt / ARN and carry it to the appointment.
Attend the PSK with all originals: Counter A does document scanning and biometrics, Counter B is verification, Counter C is granting.
Police verification follows (pre- or post-issue); track status on the portal or the mPassport Seva app.
offline_steps: There is no fully offline route — online registration and appointment booking are mandatory.
Citizens without internet access can use a MeeSeva or Common Service Centre to complete the online form and payment on their behalf.
Walk-in is allowed only in specific emergency categories at the discretion of the Regional Passport Officer.

breakdown: Fresh/re-issue, 36 pages, 10 years validity: ₹1,500
Fresh/re-issue, 60 pages, 10 years validity: ₹2,000
Minor below 15 (5 years / till 18): ₹1,000
Tatkaal 36 pages: ₹3,500
Tatkaal 60 pages: ₹4,000
Replacement of lost/damaged passport, 36 pages: ₹3,000
fees.notes: Fees are set by MEA and are the same across India.

processing_time: Normal: 30–45 days including police verification. Tatkaal: 3–7 working days.
common_issues: Appointment slots at Vijayawada and Visakhapatnam PSKs open at a fixed time and fill within minutes.
Photocopies taken on a mobile camera are rejected — bring proper photocopies.
Address mismatch between Aadhaar and the application triggers adverse police verification.

name: Learner's Licence (LLR)
description: The first stage of getting a driving licence in Andhra Pradesh. Issued by the Transport Department through Sarathi/Parivahan after an online or computer-based test at the RTA office.
eligibility: 16+ for a gearless two-wheeler under 50cc (with guardian consent), 18+ for other non-transport vehicles, 20+ for transport vehicles.
documents: Aadhaar card (enables the fully online 'Aadhaar authenticated' route)
Proof of age and address
Form 1 self-declaration of physical fitness (Form 1A medical certificate for transport vehicles or applicants over 40)
Passport-size photographs
Blood group proof
online_steps: Open the Parivahan Sarathi portal, select Andhra Pradesh and choose 'Apply for Learner Licence'.
Complete Aadhaar e-KYC — if authentication succeeds you can take the LL test from home.
Fill Form 2, select the vehicle class(es) and upload documents and photo/signature.
Pay the fee online and book a test slot at the RTA office if e-KYC was not possible.
Take the 20-question multiple-choice test on road signs and rules; the pass mark is 12 correct answers.
Download the Learner's Licence PDF immediately after passing.
offline_steps: Visit the district RTA/RTO office with filled Form 2 and documents.
Get the documents verified at the scrutiny counter and pay the fee at the cash counter.
Take the computer-based test in the test hall.
Collect the printed LL the same day if you pass.

breakdown: Learner's licence fee (Form 3): ₹150 per class of vehicle
Learner's licence test fee: ₹50
Typical all-inclusive amount paid at AP RTA counters: around ₹260
fees.notes: Amounts vary slightly with the number of vehicle classes applied for.

processing_time: Same day if the test is passed; LL is valid for 6 months
common_issues: Server downtime at AP RTA offices frequently postpones scheduled test slots.
Aadhaar-linked mobile is mandatory for the from-home test route.

name: Permanent Driving Licence
description: Permanent driving licence issued after holding a Learner's Licence for at least 30 days and passing a practical driving test at the RTA office track.
eligibility: Holder of a valid Learner's Licence, minimum 30 days and maximum 180 days old.
documents: Valid Learner's Licence
Aadhaar card
Form 4 application
Form 1A medical certificate for transport vehicles or applicants above 40
Vehicle in roadworthy condition for the driving test (with valid insurance and PUC)
Passport-size photographs
online_steps: Go to Parivahan Sarathi, select Andhra Pradesh and choose 'Apply for Driving Licence'.
Enter the LL number and date of birth to auto-fill the application.
Upload documents, photo and signature.
Pay the fee online and book a driving test slot at your RTA office.
Attend the test with your own vehicle, helmet and the appointment slip.
On passing, the smart-card DL is printed and dispatched by post; download the e-DL from the portal or DigiLocker.
offline_steps: Submit Form 4 with the LL and documents at the RTA office.
Pay the fee at the counter and get a test slot.
Take the practical driving test on the automated track.
Give biometrics and photograph for the smart card after passing.

breakdown: Driving test fee: ₹300
Smart card driving licence: ₹200
Additional class endorsement: ₹500 per class
Typical all-inclusive amount paid at AP RTA counters: around ₹960
fees.notes: Renewal costs approximately ₹200 plus late fee; international driving permit ₹1,000.

processing_time: Test result same day; smart card delivered in 15–30 days
common_issues: Card printing delays due to stationery shortage are common at AP RTA offices.
Test slots are cancelled when the automated track sensors malfunction.

name: Vehicle Registration (RC) and Ownership Transfer
description: Registration Certificate for a new vehicle (Form 20) and transfer of ownership on sale (Forms 29 and 30), handled through Vahan by the AP Transport Department.
eligibility: Owner or purchaser of a motor vehicle in Andhra Pradesh.
documents: Form 20 (new registration) or Forms 29 and 30 (transfer)
Sale certificate Form 21 and roadworthiness certificate Form 22 from the dealer
Valid insurance certificate
Invoice showing ex-showroom price
PAN card or Form 60/61
Aadhaar and address proof
Existing RC and NOC from the previous RTA if the vehicle is coming from another district or state
online_steps: Most new-vehicle registrations are filed by the dealer through the Vahan portal.
For transfers, log in to Vahan, select 'Transfer of Ownership', enter the registration and chassis number.
Upload Forms 29/30, insurance and address proof.
Pay the transfer fee and road tax difference online.
Book an inspection slot if physical verification is required.
Track the application and download the updated RC from Vahan or DigiLocker.
offline_steps: Submit the physical forms with the dealer invoice at the district RTA office.
Present the vehicle for chassis-number imprint and inspection by the Motor Vehicle Inspector.
Pay road tax and registration fee at the counter.
Collect the temporary receipt; the smart-card RC is posted later.

breakdown: Registration fee: ₹300 for two-wheelers, ₹600 for cars (approx.)
AP life tax: roughly 9%–14% of vehicle cost for two-wheelers and 12%–18% for cars, depending on price slab and whether it is a first or second vehicle
Ownership transfer fee: approximately ₹300–₹1,000 depending on vehicle class
Smart card charges: approximately ₹200
fees.notes: Life tax slabs change with state budgets — always confirm the live quote generated by Vahan.

processing_time: New RC in 7–30 days; ownership transfer in 15–30 days
common_issues: Transfer stalls if the seller has pending challans or a hypothecation entry that was never cancelled.
Inter-district movement requires an NOC from the original RTA before re-registration.

name: Income Certificate
description: Certificate issued by the Tahsildar stating the annual family income. Required for scholarships, fee reimbursement, EWS reservation, welfare schemes and many college admissions in Andhra Pradesh.
eligibility: Any resident of Andhra Pradesh. Income is assessed for the whole family unit.
documents: Aadhaar card of the applicant and family members
Ration card / rice card
Residence proof
Salary certificate or employer letter if salaried
Land holding details (Adangal / 1-B) if agricultural income
Self-declaration of income
Passport-size photograph
online_steps: Open the AP MeeSeva citizen portal (onlineap.meeseva.gov.in) or the AP Seva / VSWS portal and log in.
Search for 'Income Certificate' under Revenue Department services.
Fill the application with Aadhaar, family details and declared annual income.
Upload the supporting documents.
Pay the fee online and note the transaction / application number.
The Village or Ward Revenue Officer conducts a field enquiry and the Tahsildar approves.
Download the digitally signed certificate from the portal or collect it from the Sachivalayam.
offline_steps: Visit the nearest MeeSeva centre or Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.
Give your Aadhaar and family details to the operator and hand over the documents.
Pay the fee and collect the acknowledgement slip with the application number.
Attend the VRO/WRO field enquiry if asked.
Collect the signed certificate from the same centre or the Sachivalayam.

breakdown: MeeSeva service charge: approximately ₹35
Printing and user charges: approximately ₹10
fees.notes: Commonly quoted as ₹35 at Sachivalayams and up to ₹45 at private MeeSeva franchises.

processing_time: 7 working days under the MeeSeva SLA; often 5 days in practice
common_issues: Applying on the Telangana MeeSeva portal by mistake — AP uses ap.meeseva.gov.in, not the Telangana domain.
Applications stuck in 'Under Process' beyond 15 days should be escalated to the Mandal Revenue Officer or helpline 1100.
Validity is usually treated as one year by most institutions.

name: Caste / Community Certificate (SC, ST, BC, OBC)
description: Integrated community certificate issued by the Tahsildar confirming the applicant's caste category. Essential for reservation in education and employment, welfare schemes and scholarships.
eligibility: Residents of Andhra Pradesh belonging to a notified SC, ST, BC or OBC community.
documents: Aadhaar card
Ration card / rice card
Residence proof
Parent's or blood relative's existing caste certificate (strongest proof)
School transfer certificate or SSC memo showing caste
Self-declaration on the prescribed format
Passport-size photograph
online_steps: Log in to the AP MeeSeva citizen portal or AP Seva / VSWS portal.
Select 'Integrated Certificate' or 'Community Certificate' under Revenue services.
Enter Aadhaar, caste, sub-caste and family details.
Upload the parent's caste certificate or school records as proof of community.
Pay the fee and note the application number.
VRO field verification is followed by Tahsildar approval.
Download the digitally signed certificate.
offline_steps: Apply at a MeeSeva centre or Grama/Ward Sachivalayam with the documents.
Provide biometric authentication and pay the fee.
Cooperate with the VRO/WRO enquiry, which may include neighbour statements.
Collect the certificate from the centre once approved.

breakdown: MeeSeva service charge: approximately ₹35
User and printing charges: approximately ₹10
fees.notes: Same fee structure as the income certificate.

processing_time: 7–15 working days; ST certificates often take longer due to additional scrutiny
common_issues: Rejections are frequent when no blood relative already holds a caste certificate.
ST claims may be referred to a scrutiny committee, adding months to the timeline.
The certificate is permanent — a fresh one is not needed each year, though some institutions wrongly insist.

name: Residence / Nativity Certificate
description: Certificate confirming that the applicant is a resident or native of Andhra Pradesh. Required for local-quota admissions, state government job applications and some scholarship schemes.
eligibility: Persons residing continuously in Andhra Pradesh, typically for at least the qualifying period specified by the scheme.
documents: Aadhaar card
Ration card / rice card
Study certificates for classes 4 to 10 (the standard proof of nativity for admissions)
Electricity bill or property tax receipt as address proof
Self-declaration
Passport-size photograph
online_steps: Log in to the AP MeeSeva citizen portal or AP Seva / VSWS portal.
Choose 'Residence Certificate' or 'Nativity Certificate' under Revenue services.
Enter your Aadhaar and address details and specify the period of residence.
Upload study certificates and address proof.
Pay the fee and note the application number.
Download the signed certificate after Tahsildar approval.
offline_steps: Apply at a MeeSeva centre or Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.
Submit copies of study certificates and address proof.
Pay the fee and keep the acknowledgement.
Collect the certificate once the VRO enquiry is complete.

breakdown: MeeSeva service charge: approximately ₹35
User charges: approximately ₹10
fees.notes: Same slab as other revenue certificates.

processing_time: 7 working days under SLA
common_issues: Applicants who studied outside AP need extra documentary proof of parental residence.
Nativity for EAMCET/local-area admission uses a different set of rules from a plain residence certificate.

name: Birth Certificate
description: Legal record of birth issued by the local registrar — the Municipality or Gram Panchayat for births registered within one year, and via a Revenue Department LRBD process for late registrations.
eligibility: Any person born in Andhra Pradesh, or the parent/guardian applying on their behalf.
documents: Hospital discharge summary or doctor's certificate of birth
Parents' Aadhaar cards
Parents' marriage certificate (where applicable)
Ration card
For late registration: non-availability certificate from the Panchayat/Municipality, SSC memo, self-affidavit
online_steps: For births under one year, apply on the Civil Registration System portal or the relevant urban body portal (UBD / Puraseva) with the hospital record.
For older births, apply through the AP MeeSeva portal for 'Late Registration of Birth', which is routed to the Revenue Department.
Fill child and parent details exactly as recorded at the hospital.
Upload the discharge summary and identity documents and pay the fee.
Track the application number; download or collect the certificate.
offline_steps: For a current birth, apply directly at the Municipality or Gram Panchayat office with the doctor's certificate.
For a late registration, apply at a MeeSeva centre — you do not need to visit government offices directly.
After the LRBD certificate is issued by the Revenue Department, collect the birth certificate from the Municipality or Gram Panchayat.

breakdown: Current registration (within one year) via CRS: approximately ₹30 service charge
Late registration via MeeSeva: additional charges apply
Registration within 21 days of birth: free
fees.notes: Late registration attracts a penalty fee and, beyond one year, requires a magistrate or RDO order in some cases.

processing_time: Current registrations: about 21 days SLA. Late registration: 30–90 days.
common_issues: Name not recorded at the time of birth requires a separate name-inclusion application.
Spelling mismatches between the hospital record and Aadhaar cause rejection.

name: Death Certificate
description: Legal record of death issued by the Municipality or Gram Panchayat. Required to settle insurance claims, pensions, bank accounts, property succession and to obtain a legal heir certificate.
eligibility: Next of kin or any person with a legitimate interest in the record.
documents: Hospital death summary or medical certificate of cause of death
Cremation or burial receipt
Aadhaar of the deceased
Aadhaar of the applicant and proof of relationship
Ration card
online_steps: Register the death on the Civil Registration System portal or the urban local body portal within 21 days.
Enter the deceased's details, date, place and cause of death.
Upload the medical certificate and identity documents.
Pay the fee and note the application number.
Download the digitally signed certificate once the registrar approves.
offline_steps: Report the death at the Municipality, Gram Panchayat or Sachivalayam within 21 days.
Submit the medical certificate and cremation/burial receipt.
Pay the fee and collect the acknowledgement.
Collect the certificate from the same office, or apply through MeeSeva for a late registration.

breakdown: Registration within 21 days: free
Certificate copy: approximately ₹30
Late registration: additional penalty
fees.notes: Multiple copies are cheap and worth taking at once — banks and insurers each keep one.

processing_time: 7–21 days for a timely registration
common_issues: CRS server outages at municipal offices are a frequent cause of delay.
Deaths at home without medical attendance need a police or panchayat attestation.

name: Marriage Registration Certificate
description: Registration of marriage under the Hindu Marriage Act, Special Marriage Act or other applicable personal law, done at the Sub-Registrar Office under the AP Registration and Stamps Department (IGRS).
eligibility: Couples where the groom is 21+ and bride is 18+, with at least one party resident in the jurisdiction.
documents: Aadhaar cards of both spouses
Age proof for both (SSC memo / birth certificate / passport)
Address proof for both
Wedding invitation card and marriage photographs
Priest's or temple certificate where the marriage was solemnised religiously
Three witnesses with their Aadhaar cards
Passport-size photographs of both parties
Divorce decree or death certificate of previous spouse, if applicable
online_steps: Visit the AP Registration and Stamps (IGRS) portal and select marriage registration.
Fill the application with both spouses' details and marriage date.
Upload documents and photographs.
Book an appointment slot at the concerned Sub-Registrar Office.
Attend with both spouses and three witnesses for signature and biometric capture.
Collect or download the certificate after registration.
offline_steps: Approach the Sub-Registrar Office in whose jurisdiction the marriage took place or either party resides.
Submit the memorandum of marriage with the documents and photographs.
Appear before the Sub-Registrar with three witnesses.
For a Special Marriage Act registration, a 30-day public notice period applies before registration.
Collect the certificate after the Registrar signs it.

breakdown: Hindu Marriage Act registration: roughly ₹100–₹500
Special Marriage Act registration: roughly ₹150–₹1,000
Late registration beyond the prescribed period attracts additional fees
fees.notes: Fees vary by Act and by delay period — confirm the live quote at the Sub-Registrar Office.

processing_time: Same day to 15 days under the Hindu Marriage Act; 30–45 days under the Special Marriage Act due to the notice period
common_issues: Special Marriage Act registrations require a 30-day notice that is publicly displayed.
Sub-Registrar Offices near major junctions are heavily crowded on auspicious days.

name: Legal Heir Certificate
description: Certificate issued by the Tahsildar identifying the surviving legal heirs of a deceased person. Used for transferring bank balances, pensions, insurance claims and movable assets.
eligibility: Surviving spouse, children, parents or siblings of the deceased.
documents: Death certificate of the deceased
Aadhaar cards of all claimed legal heirs
Ration card showing family composition
Proof of relationship (birth certificates, marriage certificate)
Self-declaration affidavit listing all heirs
Address proof of the deceased
online_steps: Log in to the AP MeeSeva citizen portal or AP Seva / VSWS portal.
Select 'Family Member Certificate' or 'Legal Heir Certificate' under Revenue services.
Enter the deceased's details and list every surviving heir.
Upload the death certificate and relationship proofs.
Pay the fee and note the application number.
The VRO conducts a field enquiry and the Tahsildar issues the certificate.
offline_steps: Apply at a MeeSeva centre or Grama/Ward Sachivalayam with the death certificate.
Submit the affidavit listing all heirs.
Cooperate with the VRO enquiry, which may include a public notice.
Collect the certificate from the centre or the Tahsildar office.

breakdown: MeeSeva service charge: approximately ₹35
Affidavit stamp paper and notary: ₹50–₹200 extra
fees.notes: For disputed estates or immovable property, a succession certificate from a civil court is required instead — that is a separate and costlier process.

processing_time: 15–30 days, longer if a public notice period is applied
common_issues: A legal heir certificate is not a substitute for a court succession certificate when immovable property or contested claims are involved.
Omitting an heir invalidates the certificate and creates later disputes.

name: Land Records — Adangal, 1-B (ROR) and e-Passbook
description: Andhra Pradesh agricultural land records available on the Meebhoomi portal. The Adangal (Pahani) shows crop, soil, irrigation and cultivator details; the 1-B (Record of Rights) is the ownership record maintained by the Tahsildar. Meebhoomi is the public front-end for the Webland backend.
eligibility: Any citizen can view records; certified or digitally signed copies are issued to the pattadar.
documents: Aadhaar number, or
Khata (account) number, or
Survey number with district, mandal and village
online_steps: Open meebhoomi.ap.gov.in — there is no official Meebhoomi mobile app, so use the mobile browser.
Choose 'Your Adangal' / 'Village Adangal' or 'Me 1-B' / 'Village 1-B'.
Select district, mandal and village.
Search by Aadhaar number, khata number or survey number and enter the captcha.
View or download the report; the e-Passbook carries a digital signature and QR code and is accepted by banks and sub-registrars.
Check whether the record appears in red — red means digitally unsigned in the Webland backend and it will be rejected by banks and sub-registrars.
offline_steps: Visit the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or the Mandal Revenue Office (Tahsildar).
If the record is unsigned, submit a written application to the MRO/Tahsildar with registered sale deeds, tax receipts and Aadhaar.
The Tahsildar logs into Webland, does biometric verification and digitally signs the record.
Collect a certified copy from the Sachivalayam or a MeeSeva centre.

breakdown: Viewing and downloading from Meebhoomi: free
Certified/attested copy at MeeSeva: approximately ₹35–₹50
fees.notes: Beware of third-party apps and sites that charge for what is free on the official portal.

processing_time: Instant online; 7–30 days if the record needs to be digitally signed by the Tahsildar
common_issues: Records shown in red are digitally unsigned and cannot be used for loans, court filings or registrations.
Some private patta lands were misclassified as assigned or poramboke land during the 2015 digitisation and land up on the Section 22A prohibited-registration list.
No official government Meebhoomi mobile app exists; third-party apps raise data-privacy concerns.

name: Land Mutation (Transfer of Title in Revenue Records)
description: Updating the ownership entry in the Record of Rights after a sale, gift, partition or inheritance so that the new owner's name appears on the Adangal and 1-B. For urban property, mutation of the property tax assessment is done by the Municipality.
eligibility: The purchaser, donee or legal heir of the land.
documents: Registered sale deed, gift deed, partition deed or will
Existing Adangal and 1-B of the seller
Encumbrance certificate
Death certificate and legal heir certificate for inheritance mutation
Aadhaar of the applicant
Latest land revenue or property tax receipt
online_steps: Automatic mutation is triggered for many registered sale deeds through the linkage between the Registration Department and Webland.
Where automatic mutation does not happen, apply through the AP MeeSeva portal or the Sachivalayam for 'Mutation'.
Upload the registered deed and existing land records.
Pay the mutation fee and note the application number.
The Tahsildar issues notices to interested parties, then approves.
Verify the change on Meebhoomi and confirm the record is digitally signed.
offline_steps: Submit a written application to the MRO/Tahsildar with the registered deed and existing records.
For municipal property, apply to the Revenue Section of the Municipal Corporation.
Respond to notices served on the previous owner and neighbours.
Collect the updated 1-B or property tax demand notice after approval.

breakdown: Agricultural mutation fee: nominal, typically under ₹500
Municipal property mutation: often calculated as a small percentage of the property value or a fixed slab
fees.notes: Municipal mutation fees vary by corporation — confirm with GVMC, VMC, GMC, TMC or the relevant body.

processing_time: 30–90 days; municipal mutation often 30–45 days
common_issues: Mutation is one of the most commonly reported bribe-prone processes — escalate delays in writing to the RDO or Collector.
Mutation cannot proceed while the land is on the Section 22A prohibited list.

name: Encumbrance Certificate (EC)
description: A statement from the Sub-Registrar showing all registered transactions on a property for a requested period — sales, mortgages, gifts and leases. Banks require it for property loans and buyers require it to confirm clear title.
eligibility: Any person may apply for the EC of a property.
documents: Property survey number or door number with village/ward details
Copy of the sale deed or document number of a prior transaction
Aadhaar of the applicant
Period for which the search is required
online_steps: Open the AP Registration and Stamps (IGRS) portal and select 'Encumbrance Search (EC)'.
Enter the district, Sub-Registrar Office, village and survey or document number.
Select the period of search.
Pay the search fee online.
View and download the EC statement; a certified signed copy can be requested through MeeSeva.
offline_steps: Apply in Form 22 at the Sub-Registrar Office having jurisdiction over the property.
Pay the search and certificate fee at the counter.
Collect the certified EC after the record search is completed.

breakdown: Application fee: approximately ₹25
Search fee: charged per year of search, roughly ₹10–₹15 per year
Certified copy via MeeSeva: approximately ₹200 total for a typical 13–30 year search
fees.notes: Total depends heavily on the number of years searched.

processing_time: Online view is instant; certified copy in 3–7 working days
common_issues: An EC only covers registered documents — unregistered agreements and oral partitions will not appear.
Records before computerisation may require a manual search at the SRO.

name: Property Tax Assessment and Payment
description: New assessment, revision and payment of property tax for buildings in urban local bodies of Andhra Pradesh, administered by the Commissioner and Director of Municipal Administration (CDMA) through the Puraseva system.
eligibility: Owners of any building within a municipality or corporation limit.
documents: Registered sale deed or title document
Approved building plan and occupancy certificate
Aadhaar of the owner
Previous property tax receipt for revisions
Site plan and measurement details
online_steps: Open the AP CDMA / Puraseva portal and select your municipality or corporation.
For a new assessment, choose 'Self Assessment of Property Tax' and enter plot, plinth area and usage details.
Upload the sale deed and building plan.
Submit and note the application number; a Bill Collector inspects the property.
Once assessed, pay online with the PTIN and download the receipt.
For routine payment, search by PTIN or door number and pay the half-yearly demand.
offline_steps: Apply at the Revenue Section of the Municipal Corporation or Municipality.
Submit the title document and building plan for inspection.
The Bill Collector and Revenue Officer inspect and assign a PTIN.
Pay at the citizen service centre or a MeeSeva counter and collect the receipt.

breakdown: Tax is computed from the annual rental value, plinth area, zone and usage type
New assessment application: nominal, typically under ₹200
Rebates are usually offered for early payment in the first half-year
fees.notes: Rates differ across GVMC, VMC, GMC, TMC and KMC — always use the municipality's own calculator.

processing_time: New assessment in 15–30 days; payment is instant
common_issues: Property tax mutation after purchase is separate from revenue-record mutation — both need to be done.
Unauthorised constructions get penal assessment rates.

name: Building Permission / Plan Approval
description: Approval to construct or alter a building, issued by the urban local body or the relevant development authority through the online Development Permission Management System.
eligibility: Owner of the plot, applying through a licensed technical person (architect or engineer).
documents: Registered sale deed and link documents
Latest EC and property tax receipt
Approved layout copy or LP number
Site plan, building plan and structural drawings prepared by a licensed technical person
Land use certificate / CLU where applicable
NOC from Fire Services for buildings above the prescribed height
Aadhaar of the owner
online_steps: Register on the AP CDMA / DPMS online building permission system through your ULB.
Engage a licensed technical person who uploads the CAD drawings for automated scrutiny.
The system auto-checks setbacks, height, FAR and road width against the building rules.
Pay the assessed fees online.
Site inspection is done by the Town Planning Officer.
Download the sanctioned plan and proceedings once approved.
offline_steps: Submit the application with drawings at the Town Planning Section of the ULB.
Pay the scrutiny and development charges at the counter.
Cooperate with the site inspection.
Collect the sanctioned plan and building permit.

breakdown: Site and scrutiny fees based on plot area
Building permit fee based on built-up area
Development charges, betterment charges and labour cess
Open space contribution for larger plots
fees.notes: This is the most variable fee in the list — it depends on plot size, built-up area, zone and ULB. Use the DPMS auto-calculator.

processing_time: 15–60 days depending on building category; instant approval for small residential plots under the self-certification scheme
common_issues: Drawings must be uploaded by a registered licensed technical person — an owner cannot self-upload.
Deviation from the sanctioned plan leads to demolition notices or heavy penal charges.

name: Rice Card / Ration Card (New and Modification)
description: The AP Civil Supplies rice card entitles a household to subsidised food grains under the National Food Security Act and acts as the primary household identity document for most state welfare schemes.
eligibility: Households meeting the AP government's income, land holding, vehicle ownership and electricity consumption criteria. Applications are processed through the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam.
documents: Aadhaar cards of all family members
Address proof (electricity bill / rental agreement)
Income declaration
Bank passbook of the head of family
Existing ration card for modifications, surrender or splitting
Marriage certificate for adding a spouse
online_steps: Log in to the AP Seva / VSWS portal or the AP Civil Supplies (EPDS) portal.
Select the appropriate service — new rice card, member addition, member deletion, splitting or address change.
Enter Aadhaar details for every member and complete e-KYC.
Upload address proof and supporting documents.
Submit; the Volunteer and Welfare Assistant conduct a household verification.
Track the application number and download the card once approved.
offline_steps: Approach your Grama/Ward Sachivalayam with all family Aadhaar cards.
The Digital Assistant files the application and completes biometric e-KYC.
A Village/Ward Volunteer visits the house for verification.
Collect the approved rice card from the Sachivalayam or the Fair Price Shop.

breakdown: New card and modifications: free
fees.notes: No government fee. Private centres sometimes charge a handling fee.

processing_time: 30–60 days; applications are often processed in periodic government sanction cycles rather than continuously
common_issues: New cards are sanctioned in batches, so an approved application can still wait for the next issuance cycle.
Aadhaar seeding failures for elderly members with worn fingerprints block e-KYC — iris authentication is the workaround.

name: Disability Certificate (SADAREM)
description: The Software for Assessment of Disabled for Access, Rehabilitation and Empowerment issues the AP disability certificate and UDID, which unlocks disability pensions, reservations, travel concessions and scheme benefits.
eligibility: Persons with any of the 21 disabilities specified under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016.
documents: Aadhaar card
Ration card
Passport-size photographs
Existing medical records, scans or reports relating to the disability
Previous SADAREM certificate if applying for renewal or revision
online_steps: Register on the SADAREM portal or apply through the AP Seva / VSWS portal or MeeSeva.
Fill the applicant details and the nature of disability.
Book an assessment camp slot; camps are held at designated government hospitals and Area Hospitals.
Attend the medical board assessment with all reports.
Download the SADAREM certificate with the disability percentage once the board finalises it.
Use the SADAREM certificate to apply for the national UDID card on the UDID portal.
offline_steps: Apply at the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or MeeSeva centre.
Attend the SADAREM camp at the designated government hospital on the allotted date.
Undergo assessment by the medical board.
Collect the certificate from the hospital or the Sachivalayam.

breakdown: Assessment and certificate: free
fees.notes: MeeSeva may charge a small application service fee.

processing_time: 30–90 days depending on camp scheduling
common_issues: Assessment camps are held only periodically, so waiting lists build up — acknowledgements for waitlisted applicants are published on the MeeSeva portal.
The assessed percentage determines eligibility for the pension, so carrying complete medical records matters.

name: Trade Licence
description: Permission from the urban local body to carry on a trade or business at a specified premises within municipal limits, issued under the AP Municipalities Act and renewed annually.
eligibility: Any person or entity operating a shop, hotel, workshop, clinic or other trade within a municipality or corporation.
documents: Aadhaar and PAN of the proprietor or partners
Property tax receipt of the premises
Rental agreement or ownership proof of the premises
Site plan and photographs of the premises
NOC from the property owner if rented
Fire NOC and pollution consent where the trade requires it
GST registration certificate if applicable
online_steps: Open the AP CDMA / Puraseva portal and select your municipality or corporation.
Choose 'Trade Licence' → 'New Application'.
Enter business details, trade category and premises information.
Upload the property tax receipt, rental agreement and photographs.
Pay the licence fee online based on the trade category.
A Sanitary Inspector or Town Planning official inspects the premises.
Download the licence certificate once issued; renew every year before the due date.
offline_steps: Apply at the Public Health or Revenue Section of the Municipal Corporation.
Submit the application with premises documents and pay the fee at the counter.
Cooperate with the premises inspection.
Collect the trade licence certificate.

breakdown: Fee is slab-based by trade category and premises area
Typical small retail shop: roughly ₹500–₹3,000 per year
Food establishments, hotels and industrial trades attract higher slabs
Late renewal attracts penalty
fees.notes: Slabs differ across GVMC, VMC, GMC, TMC and KMC — check the ULB schedule.

processing_time: 7–30 days
common_issues: Operating without a trade licence attracts sealing notices and penalties.
Renewal lapses are common because there is no automatic reminder.

name: GST Registration
description: Registration under the Goods and Services Tax regime, giving a 15-digit GSTIN. Mandatory above the turnover threshold and for inter-state suppliers, e-commerce sellers and certain notified categories.
eligibility: Turnover above ₹40 lakh for goods and ₹20 lakh for services in most states; compulsory registration regardless of turnover for inter-state supply, e-commerce operators, casual taxable persons and reverse-charge cases.
documents: PAN of the business or proprietor
Aadhaar of the proprietor, partners or directors
Photograph of the proprietor, partners or directors
Proof of principal place of business (electricity bill, property tax receipt, rental agreement with NOC)
Bank account proof (cancelled cheque, passbook first page or bank statement)
Certificate of incorporation, partnership deed or LLP agreement as applicable
Digital signature certificate for companies and LLPs
Board resolution or authorisation letter for the authorised signatory
online_steps: Go to the GST portal and select Services → Registration → New Registration.
Complete Part A with PAN, mobile and email to receive a Temporary Reference Number (TRN).
Log in with the TRN and complete Part B: business details, promoters, authorised signatory, principal place of business, goods and services (HSN/SAC), bank account.
Upload the supporting documents.
Complete Aadhaar authentication for the authorised signatory — this avoids mandatory physical verification in most cases.
Submit with EVC or DSC and note the ARN.
Track the ARN; respond to any query in Form GST REG-03 within seven working days.
Download the GST REG-06 registration certificate with the GSTIN once approved.
offline_steps: There is no offline route — registration is fully online.
Citizens can use a GST Suvidha Provider, chartered accountant or a MeeSeva/CSC operator to file on their behalf.
If Aadhaar authentication is skipped or fails, a physical verification of premises by the jurisdictional officer follows.

breakdown: Government fee: nil
fees.notes: Professionals typically charge ₹500–₹3,000 for filing assistance.

processing_time: 7 working days with successful Aadhaar authentication; up to 30 days if physical verification is triggered
common_issues: Rental agreements without an owner NOC are the most common rejection reason.
Mismatch between the address on the electricity bill and the application triggers physical verification.

name: Shops and Establishments Registration
description: Registration under the AP Shops and Establishments Act, administered by the Labour Department. Required for any commercial establishment employing staff, and commonly demanded by banks for a current account.
eligibility: Every shop or commercial establishment in Andhra Pradesh, to be registered within 30 days of commencing business.
documents: Aadhaar and PAN of the employer
Address proof of the establishment
Rental agreement or ownership document
Details of employees with wages
Photograph of the establishment with the name board
Partnership deed or incorporation certificate as applicable
online_steps: Register on the AP Labour Department portal (LDM / Shram Suvidha for AP).
Select 'Shops and Establishments Registration' and fill the establishment details.
Enter employer, manager and employee particulars.
Upload address proof and photographs.
Pay the registration fee online based on the number of employees.
Download the registration certificate, which must be displayed at the premises.
offline_steps: Submit the application form at the office of the Assistant Commissioner of Labour for the district.
Attach establishment and employee details with the fee challan.
Collect the registration certificate after verification.

breakdown: Fee is slab-based on the number of employees
No employees: lowest slab, roughly ₹100–₹500
Larger establishments: several thousand rupees
Renewal is periodic and follows the same slab
fees.notes: Confirm current slabs on the AP Labour Department portal.

processing_time: 3–15 working days
common_issues: Many businesses skip this and later find banks refusing to open a current account.
Employee wage registers must be maintained once registered.

name: Udyam (MSME) Registration
description: Free online registration of a micro, small or medium enterprise on the Udyam portal, generating a permanent Udyam Registration Number. Unlocks collateral-free credit, priority sector lending, subsidy schemes and protection under the delayed-payment provisions.
eligibility: Any manufacturing or service enterprise within the MSME investment and turnover limits.
documents: Aadhaar of the proprietor, managing partner or authorised signatory
PAN of the enterprise
GSTIN where applicable
Bank account details
Investment and turnover figures (auto-fetched from PAN and GST data)
online_steps: Open the Udyam Registration portal and choose 'For New Entrepreneurs who are not registered yet as MSME'.
Enter the Aadhaar number and name, and verify with OTP.
Enter the PAN — turnover and investment data are pulled automatically from income tax and GST systems.
Fill enterprise details: name, type of organisation, address, bank account, NIC activity codes, number of employees.
Submit and verify with OTP.
Download the Udyam Registration Certificate with the URN and QR code immediately.
offline_steps: No offline route. Assistance is available free of charge at District Industries Centres, MSME-Development Institutes and Champions Control Rooms.

breakdown: Government fee: nil
fees.notes: The official portal is free. Numerous look-alike private sites charge for this — they are not official.

processing_time: Instant — certificate is generated on submission
common_issues: Paid look-alike websites are the biggest trap — always use udyamregistration.gov.in.
One enterprise can hold only one Udyam registration; all activities must be listed under it.

name: FSSAI Food Licence / Registration
description: Mandatory registration or licence for any food business — manufacturing, storage, transport, retail or catering — issued through the FoSCoS portal. The category depends on annual turnover.
eligibility: Basic Registration up to ₹12 lakh turnover; State Licence for ₹12 lakh–₹20 crore; Central Licence above ₹20 crore or for importers/exporters and specified categories.
documents: Aadhaar and PAN of the proprietor or partners
Passport-size photograph
Proof of premises (rental agreement or ownership document)
Layout plan of the processing unit for State and Central licences
List of food products handled
Water test report for manufacturing units
Food safety management plan for State and Central licences
NOC from the local body
online_steps: Register on the FoSCoS portal and check your eligible category using the licence category tool.
Select Basic Registration, State Licence or Central Licence.
Fill Form A (registration) or Form B (licence) with business and product details.
Upload the documents and pay the fee online.
Note the application reference number; a Food Safety Officer may inspect the premises.
Download the certificate with the 14-digit FSSAI number and display it at the premises.
offline_steps: Submit Form A or Form B at the office of the Designated Officer for the district.
Attach the documents and pay the fee by demand draft.
Cooperate with the Food Safety Officer inspection.
Collect the licence certificate.

breakdown: Basic Registration: ₹100 per year
State Licence: ₹2,000–₹5,000 per year depending on the category
Central Licence: ₹7,500 per year
Licences can be taken for 1 to 5 years at a time
fees.notes: Fees are set centrally by FSSAI.

processing_time: Basic Registration: 7–15 days. State/Central Licence: 30–60 days.
common_issues: Choosing the wrong category for the turnover leads to rejection and re-application.
The FSSAI number must be printed on all food labels and displayed at the premises.

name: Fire Safety NOC
description: No Objection Certificate from the AP State Disaster Response and Fire Services Department, required for high-rise buildings, hospitals, schools, hotels, cinemas, industrial units and other notified occupancies.
eligibility: Owners or occupiers of buildings and establishments falling within the notified categories under the AP Fire Service Act.
documents: Approved building plan
Fire safety installation drawings
Details of firefighting equipment installed
Structural stability certificate
Ownership or lease document
Aadhaar of the applicant
Previous NOC for renewal
online_steps: Apply on the AP Fire Services online NOC portal, often integrated with the building permission (DPMS) workflow.
Select provisional NOC (before construction) or final NOC (after completion).
Upload the building and fire safety drawings.
Pay the scrutiny fee online.
A Fire Officer inspects the installed systems.
Download the NOC; renewals are typically annual for occupied buildings.
offline_steps: Submit the application with drawings at the District Fire Officer's office.
Pay the fee and schedule the inspection.
Rectify any deficiencies noted by the Fire Officer.
Collect the NOC after re-inspection.

breakdown: Fee is calculated on built-up area and occupancy type
Renewal fees are lower than the initial NOC
fees.notes: Confirm the current schedule with the AP Fire Services department.

processing_time: 15–45 days including inspection
common_issues: Provisional NOC is needed before building permission for notified categories, so applying late stalls the whole project.
Annual renewal is often forgotten until an inspection drive.

name: Pollution Control Consent (CFE / CFO)
description: Consent for Establishment and Consent for Operation issued by the Andhra Pradesh Pollution Control Board under the Water and Air Acts. Required for industries and many commercial establishments, classified into Red, Orange, Green and White categories.
eligibility: Any industry or activity in the Red, Orange or Green category. White category units only need to intimate the Board.
documents: Project report with manufacturing process and capacity
Land documents and site plan
Water balance and effluent treatment details
Air pollution control equipment details
Approved building plan
Aadhaar and PAN of the applicant
Environmental clearance where the project size requires it
online_steps: Register on the APPCB online consent management system.
Select CFE for a new unit or CFO for commencing operation.
Identify the category (Red / Orange / Green / White) and capital investment slab.
Upload the project report and technical documents.
Pay the consent fee online based on category and investment.
A Regional Officer inspects the site.
Download the consent order; renew CFO before expiry.
offline_steps: Submit the application at the APPCB Regional Office for the district.
Pay the fee by demand draft.
Cooperate with the site inspection and comply with any conditions.
Collect the consent order.

breakdown: Fee slabs are based on capital investment and pollution category
Red category units pay substantially more than Green
fees.notes: Refer to the APPCB fee schedule for the exact slab.

processing_time: 30–120 days depending on category
common_issues: Operating without a valid CFO leads to closure notices and electricity disconnection.
Category classification errors cause the application to be returned.

name: New Electricity Connection
description: New service connection from the relevant AP distribution company. APEPDCL serves the northern districts including Visakhapatnam, Vizianagaram, Srikakulam, Kakinada and the Godavari districts; APSPDCL serves the southern districts including Vijayawada, Guntur, Tirupati, Nellore and Kurnool.
eligibility: Owner or occupier of the premises. Load category determines the tariff — domestic, commercial, agricultural or industrial.
documents: Aadhaar of the applicant
Ownership document or rental agreement with owner NOC
Property tax receipt or building permission copy
Wiring completion certificate from a licensed electrical contractor (Form B)
Site plan / location sketch
Passport-size photograph
For agricultural connections: land records (Adangal / 1-B)
online_steps: Open the APEPDCL or APSPDCL website depending on your district and select 'New Service Connection'.
Fill the application with premises details, required load in kW and category.
Upload ownership proof, wiring completion certificate and photograph.
Pay the application and registration charges online.
An Assistant Engineer inspects the premises and issues a demand notice for development charges and security deposit.
Pay the demand notice amount online.
The meter is installed and the connection is energised; track status with the application number.
offline_steps: Submit the application at the Section Office or Customer Service Centre of APEPDCL/APSPDCL.
Attach the wiring completion certificate and ownership proof.
Pay the registration fee at the counter.
After inspection, pay the demand notice amount.
The lineman installs the meter and releases supply.

breakdown: Application and registration charges: a few hundred rupees
Security deposit: based on connected load and category
Development charges: based on load and distance from the nearest distribution point
Typical single-phase domestic connection total: roughly ₹1,500–₹4,000
fees.notes: Costs rise sharply if a new pole or transformer is needed. Get the demand notice in writing.

processing_time: 7–30 days for a standard domestic connection where the network already exists
common_issues: Wiring completion certificate from an unlicensed electrician is rejected.
Rented premises need a written NOC from the owner.

name: New Water Supply Connection
description: Piped water supply connection provided by the urban local body — GVMC in Visakhapatnam, VMC in Vijayawada, GMC in Guntur, TMC in Tirupati and KMC in Kakinada — or by the Gram Panchayat in rural areas.
eligibility: Owner or occupier of a property within the water supply network area.
documents: Property tax receipt with PTIN
Ownership document or rental agreement with owner NOC
Aadhaar of the applicant
Approved building plan
Site sketch showing the nearest water main
Previous water bill for additional connections
online_steps: Open the AP CDMA / Puraseva portal or the corporation's own portal and select 'Water Tap Connection'.
Enter the PTIN and property details.
Choose the connection type — domestic, commercial or industrial — and pipe size.
Upload ownership proof and the building plan.
Pay the application fee online.
An Assistant Engineer inspects and issues a demand notice for donation and deposit charges.
Pay the demand and the connection is laid; download the receipt and connection number.
offline_steps: Apply at the Water Works section of the Municipal Corporation or at the ward office.
Submit property documents and pay the application fee.
After inspection, pay the donation and road-cutting charges.
The section staff lay the connection and fix the meter.

breakdown: Application fee: a few hundred rupees
Water donation / capital contribution charge: varies significantly by ULB and pipe size
Road cutting and restoration charges where applicable
Meter cost
fees.notes: GVMC, VMC and other corporations each publish their own schedule — figures differ widely.

processing_time: 15–45 days
common_issues: Property tax arrears block a new water connection.
Road-cutting permission adds significantly to both cost and timeline.

name: New LPG Gas Connection
description: Domestic cooking gas connection from Indian Oil, HP Gas or Bharat Gas. Households below the poverty line can get a free connection under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana.
eligibility: Any household without an existing LPG connection in the same name. Ujjwala is for adult women from eligible poor households.
documents: Aadhaar card
Address proof (ration card / electricity bill / rental agreement / Aadhaar)
Bank account details for subsidy transfer
Passport-size photograph
For Ujjwala: ration card, SECC/BPL identification and self-declaration
online_steps: Visit the website of Indian Oil, HP Gas or Bharat Gas, or use the common mylpg.in portal.
Select 'New Connection' and choose the nearest distributor.
Fill personal and address details and upload Aadhaar and address proof.
Complete the KYC form and e-sign or submit for distributor verification.
Pay the security deposit online or at the distributor.
Collect the cylinder, regulator and subscription voucher from the distributor.
For Ujjwala, apply on the PMUY portal and select the connection type.
offline_steps: Visit the nearest authorised LPG distributor with Aadhaar and address proof.
Fill the KYC form and submit the documents.
Pay the security deposit and collect the receipt.
Take delivery of the cylinder, regulator and Domestic Gas Consumer Card.

breakdown: Refundable security deposit for a 14.2 kg cylinder: approximately ₹2,200
Regulator deposit: approximately ₹250
Installation, inspection and DGCC booklet charges: a few hundred rupees
Ujjwala beneficiaries: deposit waived; stove and first refill support as per the scheme
fees.notes: Deposits are revised by oil marketing companies from time to time.

processing_time: 3–15 days
common_issues: Only one subsidised connection is allowed per household under one address.
Aadhaar must be seeded with the LPG ID for subsidy transfer.

name: Jan Dhan Bank Account (PMJDY)
description: Zero-balance basic savings bank deposit account under the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, with a RuPay debit card, accident insurance cover and an overdraft facility after satisfactory operation.
eligibility: Any Indian resident aged 10 or above without an existing bank account. Minors operate jointly with a guardian.
documents: Aadhaar card (sufficient on its own for full KYC)
If no Aadhaar: any officially valid document such as Voter ID, PAN, passport, driving licence or NREGA card
If no OVD at all: a small account can be opened with a self-attested photograph and signature, valid for 12 months
Two passport-size photographs
online_steps: Download the account opening form from the PMJDY portal or the bank's website.
Many banks allow full video-KYC account opening through their app using Aadhaar and PAN.
Fill personal details and nominee information.
Complete Aadhaar e-KYC with OTP or video verification.
The account number and RuPay card details are issued digitally; the physical card arrives by post.
offline_steps: Visit any bank branch, Business Correspondent (Bank Mitra) or the India Post Payments Bank counter.
Fill the PMJDY account opening form with nominee details.
Submit Aadhaar and complete biometric authentication.
Collect the passbook and RuPay debit card.

breakdown: Account opening: free
Minimum balance: nil
RuPay debit card: free
fees.notes: Accident insurance cover is bundled with the RuPay card subject to usage conditions.

processing_time: Same day to 3 working days
common_issues: The RuPay accident cover requires the card to have been used within the specified period before the claim.
Overdraft is available only after six months of satisfactory account operation.

name: Ayushman Bharat Card (PM-JAY)
description: Health cover of ₹5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalisation under Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY, usable at empanelled public and private hospitals. Senior citizens aged 70 and above are covered under the Vay Vandana extension regardless of income.
eligibility: Families identified through the SECC deprivation criteria or the state's own list; all citizens aged 70+ under the Vay Vandana expansion.
documents: Aadhaar card
Ration card
Mobile number linked to Aadhaar
Family details as recorded in the beneficiary database
online_steps: Open the PM-JAY beneficiary portal or the Ayushman app and log in with your mobile number and OTP.
Search for your name using Aadhaar, ration card, family ID or mobile number in your district.
If listed, complete e-KYC using Aadhaar OTP or face authentication.
Upload a photograph if prompted.
The card is approved and can be downloaded immediately or after verification.
Present the card with Aadhaar at the Ayushman Mitra desk in any empanelled hospital.
offline_steps: Visit a Common Service Centre, Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or the Ayushman Mitra desk at an empanelled hospital.
Provide Aadhaar and ration card for the beneficiary search.
Complete biometric e-KYC.
Collect the printed card.

breakdown: Card and treatment: free for eligible beneficiaries
fees.notes: CSCs may charge a small printing fee. Treatment at empanelled hospitals is cashless.

processing_time: Same day if the name is already in the beneficiary list
common_issues: Names missing from the beneficiary database cannot be added on demand — the list is drawn from SECC and state data.
In Andhra Pradesh the state health scheme and PM-JAY are converged, so entitlements may be delivered through the state scheme.

name: AP State Health Scheme Card (Aarogyasri / NTR Vaidya Seva)
description: Andhra Pradesh's flagship cashless health scheme covering a large list of medical and surgical procedures at empanelled network hospitals. The scheme has been renamed across successive governments — most recently branded NTR Vaidya Seva, previously YSR Aarogyasri and originally Rajiv Aarogyasri — so citizens and staff use several names for the same entitlement.
eligibility: Households holding a valid AP rice card / white ration card and meeting the income criteria notified by the state.
documents: Rice card / white ration card
Aadhaar card of the patient
Health card if already issued
Referral or diagnosis from a government hospital or a health camp, where required
online_steps: Check eligibility and empanelled hospital lists on the AP state health scheme portal.
Apply for or download the health card by entering the rice card number and Aadhaar.
For treatment, approach the Aarogyamitra / Vaidya Mitra help desk at an empanelled hospital.
The help desk raises a pre-authorisation request in the scheme portal.
Once approved, treatment is cashless; track the case number through the portal.
offline_steps: Visit the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam or a village health clinic to get the health card issued or linked.
For treatment, go directly to the Aarogyamitra desk at any empanelled network hospital with the rice card and Aadhaar.
The Aarogyamitra handles the pre-authorisation and admission paperwork.
Post-discharge, follow-up medication and transport support may also be provided under the scheme.

breakdown: Card and covered treatment: free at empanelled hospitals
fees.notes: Only listed procedures at empanelled hospitals are covered; check the current procedure list before admission.

processing_time: Card issue same day to 15 days; pre-authorisation for treatment usually within 24 hours
common_issues: The scheme name and portal URL have changed with governments — search for the current official domain rather than relying on an old bookmark.
Only procedures on the approved list are covered; anything outside it is billed normally.

name: Social Security Pension (Old Age, Widow, Disability, Weaver and Others)
description: Monthly social security pension delivered by the AP Department of Rural Development through the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam and Volunteer network, covering old age, widows, persons with disabilities, single women, weavers, toddy tappers, transgender persons, dialysis patients and other notified categories. The programme has been branded YSR Pension Kanuka and NTR Bharosa under different governments.
eligibility: Category-specific age and income criteria; households must generally hold a rice card and meet land, income and vehicle-ownership limits.
documents: Aadhaar card
Rice card / ration card
Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-seeded)
SADAREM certificate for disability pension
Death certificate of the husband for widow pension
Age proof for old age pension
Category-specific proof such as weaver identity or dialysis records
online_steps: Apply through the AP Seva / VSWS portal by selecting the pension category.
Enter Aadhaar and household details and complete e-KYC.
Upload the category-specific supporting document.
Submit; the Welfare Assistant and Volunteer verify the household.
Track the application number on the portal; approved pensions are disbursed at the doorstep by the Volunteer or credited to the bank account.
offline_steps: Approach the Grama/Ward Sachivalayam with Aadhaar, rice card and the category document.
The Welfare Assistant files the application and completes biometric authentication.
The Village/Ward Volunteer conducts household verification.
Once sanctioned, the pension is delivered monthly at the doorstep, typically on the first of the month.

breakdown: Application: free
fees.notes: Pension amounts differ by category and are revised by the state from time to time.

processing_time: 30–90 days; sanctions are released in periodic batches
common_issues: Pensions are cancelled during periodic eligibility re-verification if the household exceeds land, income or vehicle criteria.
Aadhaar-seeded bank accounts are essential; unseeded accounts cause payment failures.

name: Farmer ID (AgriStack Farmer Registry)
description: A unique digital Farmer ID created under the AgriStack Farmer Registry by linking Aadhaar with land records. It is becoming the gateway identifier for central and state agriculture benefits, including PM-KISAN instalments, crop insurance and input subsidies.
eligibility: Farmers holding land recorded in the Andhra Pradesh land records system.
documents: Aadhaar card with a linked active mobile number
Land records — Adangal / 1-B showing khata and survey numbers
Bank account passbook
Mobile number for OTP
online_steps: Open the AgriStack Farmer Registry portal for Andhra Pradesh and register with your mobile number.
Complete Aadhaar e-KYC with OTP or face authentication.
The system fetches your land parcels from the state land record database; verify and confirm each parcel.
Add or correct social category, bank account and contact details.
Submit for approval by the village-level agriculture functionary.
Download the Farmer ID once generated; use it for PM-KISAN and other scheme linkages.
offline_steps: Attend a Farmer Registry camp at the Rythu Bharosa Kendra (RBK) or Grama Sachivalayam.
Provide Aadhaar and land details to the Village Agriculture Assistant.
Complete biometric authentication and confirm the land parcel list.
Collect the acknowledgement and check the ID status later.

breakdown: Registration: free
fees.notes: No government fee. Registration camps are conducted free of charge at RBKs.

processing_time: Same day to 15 days after verification
common_issues: Land parcels do not appear if the Adangal record is digitally unsigned in Webland — get it signed by the Tahsildar first.
Tenant farmers without recorded land rights face difficulties; a Crop Cultivator Rights Card may be needed.

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Short-term crop loan facility at concessional interest through a revolving credit card, available to farmers, tenant farmers, sharecroppers and those in animal husbandry and fisheries.
eligibility: Owner cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, sharecroppers, self-help group members and allied-activity farmers.
documents: Aadhaar card
Land records — Adangal / 1-B or Crop Cultivator Rights Card for tenants
Bank account details
Passport-size photographs
PAN card for higher loan amounts
Crop plan and acreage details
online_steps: Apply through the PM-KISAN portal's KCC link or directly on your bank's website.
Fill the KCC application with land, crop and bank details.
Upload land records and Aadhaar.
Submit; the branch verifies the land record with the revenue department.
Sign the loan documents at the branch and receive the KCC RuPay card.
offline_steps: Approach your bank branch or the Rythu Bharosa Kendra with land records and Aadhaar.
Fill the KCC application form provided by the branch.
The branch verifies land records and assesses the scale of finance for your crop.
Execute the loan documents and collect the KCC card and passbook.

breakdown: Processing fee is waived for loans up to ₹3 lakh under the interest subvention scheme
Interest subvention and prompt repayment incentive reduce the effective rate substantially
Documentation and inspection charges may apply above ₹3 lakh
fees.notes: Interest subvention rates are notified annually by the Government of India.

processing_time: 14 days as per RBI guidance for complete applications
common_issues: Tenant farmers are frequently refused despite being eligible — a Crop Cultivator Rights Card strengthens the case.
Existing loan defaults in the same household block fresh KCC sanction.

name: Crop Insurance (PMFBY)
description: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana provides insurance against crop loss from natural calamities, pests and disease. In Andhra Pradesh enrolment is closely linked to e-Crop booking done at the Rythu Bharosa Kendra.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers.
documents: Aadhaar card
Land records — Adangal / 1-B
e-Crop booking record for the season
Bank account passbook
Sowing certificate or self-declaration
Tenancy agreement for tenant farmers
online_steps: Ensure your crop is correctly recorded in the AP e-Crop booking system at the RBK — claims are settled against this record.
Enrol on the PMFBY national portal or through your bank if you have a crop loan.
Select the season, crop, and area sown.
Pay the farmer's share of the premium online.
Note the policy number and download the certificate.
For a loss event, intimate within 72 hours through the Crop Insurance app, the toll-free number or your bank.
offline_steps: Visit the Rythu Bharosa Kendra or your bank branch before the cut-off date for the season.
Verify the e-Crop entry matches the crop actually sown.
Submit the enrolment form and pay the premium share.
Collect the acknowledgement.
Report crop loss to the RBK, agriculture officer or insurer within 72 hours of the event.

breakdown: Farmer's premium share: 2% of sum insured for kharif food and oilseed crops
1.5% for rabi food and oilseed crops
5% for commercial and horticultural crops
The balance is subsidised by the central and state governments
fees.notes: Andhra Pradesh has at times borne the farmer share entirely for enrolled farmers — check the current season's notification.

processing_time: Enrolment before the seasonal cut-off; claim settlement typically within 2–3 months of yield data finalisation
common_issues: A wrong crop entry in e-Crop booking is the leading cause of claim rejection — verify it at the RBK before the cut-off.
Losses must be intimated within 72 hours for localised calamity claims.

name: e-Shram Card (Unorganised Worker Registration)
description: National database registration for unorganised sector workers, generating a 12-digit Universal Account Number. Provides accident insurance cover and acts as the gateway to several central welfare schemes.
eligibility: Unorganised workers aged 16 to 59 who are not members of EPFO or ESIC and are not income tax payers.
documents: Aadhaar card with a linked active mobile number
Bank account details (account number and IFSC)
Occupation and skill details
Nominee details
online_steps: Open the e-Shram portal and select 'Register on e-Shram'.
Enter the Aadhaar-linked mobile number and verify with OTP.
Complete Aadhaar e-KYC using OTP, fingerprint or iris.
Fill personal, address, education, occupation, skill and bank details.
Add nominee details.
Submit and download the e-Shram card with the UAN immediately.
offline_steps: Visit a Common Service Centre, MeeSeva centre or the State Seva Kendra.
Provide Aadhaar and complete biometric authentication.
Give occupation, skill and bank details to the operator.
Collect the printed e-Shram card.

breakdown: Registration: free at CSCs and on the self-registration portal
fees.notes: CSCs are reimbursed by the government and should not charge the worker.

processing_time: Instant — the card is generated on submission
common_issues: Aadhaar must be linked to an active mobile number; otherwise you must use a CSC for biometric registration.
EPFO or ESIC members are not eligible and their registration will be rejected.

name: EPF UAN Activation and Withdrawal
description: Activation of the Universal Account Number for Employees' Provident Fund, KYC seeding, transfer of accounts between employers and online claim submission for withdrawal or advances.
eligibility: Employees of establishments covered under the EPF Act.
documents: UAN allotted by the employer
Aadhaar card linked to the mobile number
PAN card
Bank account with IFSC, in the member's own name
Cancelled cheque or passbook copy
Form 15G/15H where applicable for tax exemption
online_steps: Go to the EPFO Member e-Sewa portal and click 'Activate UAN'.
Enter the UAN, Aadhaar, name and date of birth and verify with OTP.
Set a password and log in.
Under KYC, seed Aadhaar, PAN and bank details and get them approved by the employer.
Use 'One Member One EPF Account' to transfer past accounts to the current employer.
For withdrawal or advance, file the online claim (Form 31 for advance, Form 19 for final settlement, Form 10C for pension withdrawal).
Track the claim status on the portal or the UMANG app.
