offline_steps: Submit the composite claim form at the regional EPFO office with employer attestation where required.
Attach the cancelled cheque and identity documents.
Track using the claim acknowledgement.

breakdown: All EPFO member services: free
fees.notes: No fee. Beware of agents charging for withdrawal claims.

processing_time: UAN activation instant; online claims settled typically in 7–20 days
common_issues: Claims are rejected when the name, date of birth or father's name differs between EPFO records and Aadhaar.
Bank account must be in the member's sole name and Aadhaar-seeded.

name: Building and Construction Worker Registration (BOCW)
description: Registration with the AP Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, giving access to accident and death relief, maternity benefit, education assistance for children, marriage assistance and pension.
eligibility: Construction workers aged 18 to 60 who have worked at least 90 days in the preceding twelve months.
documents: Aadhaar card
Employment certificate from the employer, contractor or a registered trade union confirming 90 days of work
Bank account passbook
Passport-size photographs
Age proof
Family details and nominee declaration
online_steps: Register on the AP Labour Department portal and select the BOCW Welfare Board registration service.
Fill personal, work and nominee details.
Upload the 90-day employment certificate and Aadhaar.
Pay the registration and annual contribution.
Track the application; download the worker identity card once approved.
Apply for individual benefits (education, marriage, maternity, accident) through the same portal after registration.
offline_steps: Apply at the office of the Assistant Labour Officer or through a registered trade union.
Submit the employment certificate and documents.
Pay the registration fee and contribution.
Collect the worker identity card and pass book.

breakdown: Nominal registration fee plus a small annual contribution, typically well under ₹100 per year
fees.notes: Renewal of contribution is essential to keep benefits active.

processing_time: 15–45 days
common_issues: Benefits lapse if the annual contribution is not paid.
Getting the 90-day employment certificate is the main practical obstacle for daily wage workers.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship / Fee Reimbursement (Jnanabhumi)
description: Andhra Pradesh's integrated scholarship and tuition fee reimbursement platform covering post-matric students from SC, ST, BC, EBC, Kapu, minority and disabled categories, administered through the Jnanabhumi portal.
eligibility: Students admitted to recognised institutions in AP, meeting category, income, attendance and course criteria. Family income limits and land holding limits apply.
documents: Aadhaar card of the student and parents
Income certificate (MeeSeva)
Caste certificate (MeeSeva)
Rice card / ration card
Bank account passbook of the student, Aadhaar-seeded
Admission proof and fee receipt
Previous year marks memo
Bonafide certificate from the institution
online_steps: The institution registers the student's admission on the Jnanabhumi portal.
Log in with the Aadhaar number and application ID and complete e-KYC.
Verify the auto-fetched income and caste certificate details.
Enter bank account and course details.
Submit; the institution and the district welfare officer verify the application.
Attendance is tracked biometrically and affects disbursement.
Track the sanction and payment status on the portal.
offline_steps: Submit the documents to your college scholarship section, which files the application on your behalf.
Obtain the income and caste certificates from a MeeSeva centre first.
Follow up with the college and the district welfare office for verification.

breakdown: Application: free
Income and caste certificates from MeeSeva: approximately ₹35–₹45 each
fees.notes: The scholarship itself is a benefit, not a paid service.

processing_time: Sanction and disbursement typically follow the academic year cycle and can take several months
common_issues: Income certificate older than the current academic year is rejected.
Low biometric attendance is a common reason for withheld disbursement.
Bank account must be in the student's own name and Aadhaar-seeded.

name: Right to Information (RTI) Application
description: Application under the Right to Information Act, 2005 seeking information from a public authority. Central government bodies accept applications on the RTI Online portal; Andhra Pradesh state departments are approached through the concerned Public Information Officer, with appeals to the AP Information Commission.
eligibility: Any citizen of India. No reason for seeking the information needs to be given.
documents: Identity proof (only required to claim the BPL fee exemption)
BPL ration card if claiming exemption from the fee
A clearly worded set of questions
online_steps: For central public authorities, use the RTI Online portal, select the ministry or department and submit the request.
Pay the ₹10 fee online and note the registration number.
For AP state departments, check whether the department accepts online RTI; otherwise send a written application.
Track the status with the registration number.
If no reply arrives within 30 days, file a First Appeal to the First Appellate Authority, then a Second Appeal to the Information Commission.
offline_steps: Write a plain application addressed to the Public Information Officer of the concerned office.
State the specific information sought, with your name and postal address.
Attach an Indian Postal Order, demand draft or court fee stamp for ₹10, or a BPL card copy for exemption.
Submit in person against acknowledgement or send by registered post.
Wait 30 days for a reply (48 hours where life or liberty is involved).

breakdown: Application fee: ₹10
Photocopy charges: ₹2 per A4 page
Inspection of records: free for the first hour, then a small hourly charge
BPL cardholders: fully exempt
fees.notes: Fees are statutory and cannot be varied by the office.

processing_time: 30 days for a reply; 48 hours where life or liberty is concerned; 35 days if routed through an Assistant PIO
common_issues: Applications are rejected for being vague — ask for specific documents, file notings or figures rather than opinions.
The RTI Online portal covers central bodies; many state departments still need a physical application.

name: Police Clearance Certificate (PCC)
description: Certificate confirming the absence of adverse criminal records, issued through Passport Seva for residency, employment or long-term visa purposes abroad. AP Police also issue local police verification certificates for domestic purposes.
eligibility: Indian passport holders applying for emigration, employment or long-term visas. Not issued for tourist visas.
documents: Original passport with self-attested copies of the first two and last two pages
Aadhaar card
Proof of present address if different from the passport address
Valid visa or employment offer letter where applicable
ECR/ECNR page copy
online_steps: Log in to the Passport Seva portal and select 'Apply for Police Clearance Certificate'.
Fill the application with your passport and current address details.
Pay the fee and schedule an appointment at the PSK or POPSK.
Attend with originals for verification.
Police verification is conducted by the local station unless already cleared recently.
Collect or receive the PCC by post; track status on the portal.
offline_steps: For domestic police verification, apply on the AP Police citizen services portal or at the local police station.
Submit identity and address proof.
The Station House Officer verifies records and issues the certificate.
For overseas purposes, the Passport Seva route is the only valid one.

breakdown: PCC through Passport Seva: ₹500
Local police verification certificate: nominal or free
fees.notes: MEA-set fee, uniform across India.

processing_time: 7–30 days depending on how quickly police verification is completed
common_issues: PCC is issued for the address on record — a recent address change triggers fresh verification and delays.
It is not issued for tourist visa applications.

name: Police Complaint / e-FIR
description: Filing a complaint with Andhra Pradesh Police, including online e-FIR for property theft and vehicle theft, lost article reports, and general petitions through the AP Police citizen services platform.
eligibility: Any person aggrieved by or with knowledge of an offence.
documents: Aadhaar or other identity proof of the complainant
Details of the incident: date, time, place, description
Vehicle RC and insurance for vehicle theft
Purchase invoice or IMEI for stolen property
Supporting evidence such as photographs or messages
online_steps: Open the AP Police citizen services portal or the AP Police Seva app.
Register with your mobile number.
Select the service — e-FIR for theft, lost article report, complaint petition or cyber crime.
Fill the incident details precisely and upload any evidence.
Submit and note the acknowledgement or FIR number.
For cyber financial fraud, also report on the National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal or call 1930 within the golden hour.
Download the FIR copy or lost article certificate from the portal.
offline_steps: Visit the police station having jurisdiction over the place of the incident.
Give a written complaint; the SHO must register an FIR for a cognisable offence.
Collect a free copy of the FIR — this is your legal right.
If the station refuses, escalate in writing to the Superintendent of Police or file a complaint before the Magistrate under Section 156(3) CrPC / the corresponding BNSS provision.
Some MeeSeva centres also file lost-article complaints on your behalf.

breakdown: Filing a complaint or FIR: free
MeeSeva assistance for lost article reports: approximately ₹35
fees.notes: Police cannot charge for registering an FIR.

processing_time: e-FIR acknowledgement is immediate; investigation timelines vary
common_issues: e-FIR is available only for specified offences such as unknown-accused theft; other offences need a station visit.
For online financial fraud, reporting within the first hour materially improves the chance of freezing the funds.

name: Public Grievance Redressal (PGRS / Spandana)
description: Andhra Pradesh's public grievance system, historically run as Spandana with Monday grievance days at Collectorates and Sachivalayams, alongside the central CPGRAMS portal for grievances against central government bodies.
eligibility: Any citizen with a grievance against a government department or official.
documents: Aadhaar card
Written statement of the grievance
Copies of any earlier applications, acknowledgements or rejection orders
Supporting evidence
online_steps: Register the grievance on the AP grievance portal or through the AP Seva / VSWS portal.
Select the concerned department and grievance category.
Describe the issue and attach supporting documents.
Note the grievance registration number and track it online.
If unresolved, escalate to the next level or approach the Collector during the weekly grievance day.
For central departments, file on CPGRAMS instead.
offline_steps: Attend the weekly grievance day at the District Collectorate, Revenue Divisional Office or Sachivalayam.
Submit the written grievance and collect the acknowledgement with the reference number.
Follow up with the concerned department using the reference number.
Escalate to the Collector or the department head if the deadline passes.

breakdown: Filing a grievance: free
fees.notes: No fee at any stage.

processing_time: Departmental SLAs vary; most grievance systems target 15–30 days
common_issues: Grievances marked 'disposed' without an actual resolution should be re-opened or escalated, not re-filed as new.
Attach the earlier acknowledgement number so the history is visible to the reviewing officer.

name: No Objection Certificate (NOC) — General
description: An umbrella term for clearances issued by different authorities: vehicle NOC from the RTA for inter-district or inter-state transfer, fire NOC, pollution NOC, layout NOC, and property-owner NOC for tenants. The issuing authority depends entirely on the purpose.
eligibility: Depends on the type — vehicle owner, property owner, business operator or applicant seeking a departmental clearance.
documents: Vehicle NOC: RC, insurance, PUC, Form 28, clearance of pending challans and hypothecation NOC from the financier
Property owner NOC: ownership document, identity proof, purpose statement
Departmental NOC: application form and the documents specific to that department
online_steps: For a vehicle NOC, log in to the Parivahan Vahan portal, select 'NOC to other state/RTO' and file Form 28.
Clear all pending challans and tax dues first.
Pay the fee online and track the application.
Download the NOC once the RTA approves it.
For fire, pollution or building NOCs, use the respective department portal listed against those services.
offline_steps: Submit Form 28 in triplicate at the RTA office with the RC, insurance and tax receipts.
Obtain a hypothecation clearance letter from the financier if the vehicle is under loan.
Collect the NOC after verification, usually within a few weeks.
For a property-owner NOC, get a simple signed letter from the owner with an identity proof copy — no government process is involved.

breakdown: Vehicle NOC: roughly ₹100–₹500 plus any pending tax
Departmental NOCs: as per that department's schedule
fees.notes: The fee is entirely dependent on which NOC is being sought.

processing_time: Vehicle NOC: 15–30 days. Others vary by department.
common_issues: A vehicle NOC lapses if not used within six months, requiring a fresh application.
Pending challans anywhere in the country block the NOC.
